trends.name: Legacy-Barrieren & Migration
trends.evidence_md: More than 75% of enterprises now use AI for modernization [Riseup Labs](https://riseuplabs.com/legacy-system-modernization-trends/). Nearly 60% of AI leaders view legacy-system integration as the primary barrier to agentic AI adoption. GenAI cuts modernization timelines by up to 50%, handling 69–75% of code edits [Devox Software](https://devoxsoftware.com/blog/the-2026-legacy-modernization-report-research-insights-and-strategic-roadmap/).
trends.implications_md: Utilities with 20+ year IT landscapes face acute migration challenges, as OT systems often lack API compatibility.
trends.opportunities_md: AI-assisted migration reduces risk and timeline for legacy modernization.
trends.actions_md:
1. Develop legacy modernization strategy.
2. Leverage GenAI tools.
3. Implement phased migration.

trends.name: AI/ML-Ingenieur Einstellung 88%
trends.evidence_md: AI/ML hiring grew 88% YoY, representing 45% of all AI/ML job titles [Second Talent](https://www.secondtalent.com/resources/most-in-demand-ai-engineering-skills-and-salary-ranges/). AI talent demand exceeds supply by 3.2:1 globally, with 1.6M open positions vs. 518K candidates. AI roles command 67% higher salaries [Ravio](https://ravio.com/blog/ai-compensation-and-talent-trends).
trends.implications_md: Utilities compete with tech companies for scarce AI talent.
trends.opportunities_md: Utilities differentiate through purpose-driven career narratives.
trends.actions_md:
1. Develop competitive AI talent acquisition.
2. Build internal upskilling programs.
3. Establish university partnerships.

trends.name: API-basierte Legacy-Modernisierung
trends.evidence_md: API-based modernization enables system integration without complete rewrite. API-first architectures gaining traction across the energy sector. [No quantitative data available]
trends.opportunities_md: API gateways create integration fabric for digital utility platforms.
trends.actions_md:
1. Implement API gateways.
2. Deploy service mesh architectures.
3. Build IT/OT integration platforms.

trends.name: Talentlücke 310.000 bis 2030
trends.evidence_md: The power industry may need more than 750,000 new workers by 2030. More than 30% of utility employees are within 5 years of retirement, with retirements outnumbering new entrants by 1.7:1 [Power Magazine](https://www.powermag.com/bridging-the-gap-how-the-power-industry-is-tackling-its-workforce-crisis/). Digital roles projected to grow 30%+ through 2030.
trends.implications_md: The converging talent shortage and retirement wave force simultaneous automation and workforce development.
trends.opportunities_md: Automation compensates for workforce shortages while upskilling prepares staff for digital roles.
trends.actions_md:
1. Develop automation strategies.
2. Build remote work models.
3. Launch apprenticeship programs.

trends.name: Cloud Migration & Data Lakehouse
trends.evidence_md: Multi-cloud strategies with data lakehouse architectures enable flexible scaling and advanced analytics. [No quantitative data available]
trends.implications_md: Cloud migration requires careful workload assessment and data sovereignty compliance.
trends.opportunities_md: Cloud-native architectures enable advanced analytics and AI model training.
trends.actions_md:
1. Assess workloads for cloud readiness.
2. Implement data lakehouse.
3. Deploy hybrid cloud with sovereign options.

trends.name: Einheitliche Datenplattformen
trends.evidence_md: Unified data platforms integrating IT/OT data for real-time grid intelligence are emerging as critical by 2027–2028 [IBM](https://www.ibm.com/thought-leadership/institute-business-value/en-us/report/utilities-operations-transformed).
trends.implications_md: IT/OT data integration requires standardized models and governance frameworks.
trends.opportunities_md: Unified data platforms enable cross-functional analytics and AI-driven decisions.
trends.actions_md:
1. Implement data lakehouse with IT/OT integration.
2. Build real-time analytics platforms.
3. Establish data governance.

trends.name: Kulturwandel-Anforderung 87%
trends.evidence_md: 87% of energy/utilities professionals identify culture evolution as essential for digital transformation [Strategy&](https://www.strategyand.pwc.com/gx/en/industries/power-utilities/utilities-innovation.html).
trends.implications_md: Cultural transformation requires executive sponsorship and sustained investment.
trends.opportunities_md: Successfully transformed culture accelerates digital adoption.
trends.actions_md:
1. Conduct culture assessments.
2. Develop change management programs.
3. Build digital mindset programs.

trends.name: Offene Standards IEC 61850+
trends.evidence_md: Open standards like IEC 61850 enable multi-vendor interoperability. [No quantitative data available]
trends.implications_md: Standards adoption requires vendor alignment and testing infrastructure.
trends.opportunities_md: Open standards reduce total cost of ownership.
trends.actions_md:
1. Adopt IEC 61850.
2. Update procurement policies.
3. Invest in conformance testing.

trends.name: Digital Utility Markt €239B 2028
trends.evidence_md: The global digital power utility market is projected to grow from $55.02 billion in 2021 to $239.38 billion by 2028, at a CAGR of 23.4% [Fortune Business Insights](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/industry-reports/digital-power-utility-market-100959).
trends.implications_md: The €239B market signals massive digitalization investment globally.
trends.opportunities_md: Technology providers with utility-specific solutions can capture significant share.
trends.actions_md:
1. Benchmark digitalization investments.
2. Identify priority technology areas.
3. Build vendor partnerships.

trends.name: Master Data Management & Governance
trends.evidence_md: MDM and governance frameworks ensure data quality and single source of truth. [No quantitative data available]
trends.implications_md: MDM requires organizational alignment and data stewardship.
trends.opportunities_md: Strong data governance enables AI deployment and compliance.
trends.actions_md:
1. Implement MDM platforms.
2. Establish data stewardship.
3. Build governance processes.

trends.name: $713B Grid-Digitalisierung
trends.evidence_md: Global investment of $713 billion in grid digitalization over 6 years [Datacenters.com](https://www.datacenters.com/news/the-future-of-technology-infrastructure-trends-to-watch-through-2026).
trends.implications_md: $713B creates unprecedented demand for digital solutions.
trends.opportunities_md: Long-term revenue streams from grid modernization programs.
trends.actions_md:
1. Evaluate investment priorities.
2. Develop return models.
3. Build financing structures.

trends.name: Mitarbeiter-Pensionierungswelle
trends.evidence_md: 17% of utilities workforce retiring in next 5 years, with retirements outnumbering new entrants by 1.7:1 [consult.energy](https://consult.energy/article/hiring-trends-and-challenges-in-utilities-recruitment-in-2026). More than 30% within 5 years of retirement.
trends.implications_md: Knowledge capture and transfer become critical.
trends.opportunities_md: AI-powered knowledge management preserves institutional knowledge.
trends.actions_md:
1. Develop knowledge capture strategies.
2. Implement succession planning.
3. Build automation roadmaps.

trends.name: Edge Computing Netzoperationen
trends.evidence_md: Edge computing at grid nodes enables latency-free control by 2032–2034. [No quantitative data available]
trends.implications_md: Edge computing requires distributed infrastructure and edge AI.
trends.opportunities_md: Edge computing enables real-time grid control.
trends.actions_md:
1. Deploy edge nodes.
2. Develop edge AI.
3. Build local analytics.

trends.name: KI & Digital Twins Echtzeit
trends.evidence_md: AI + Digital Twins enable real-time grid optimization by 2030–2032 [Microsoft](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/industry/blog/energy-and-resources/power-and-utilities/2026/02/17/dtech-2026-how-microsoft-and-our-partners-are-accelerating-ai-innovation-for-utilities/).
trends.implications_md: Real-time digital twins require comprehensive sensor networks.
trends.opportunities_md: Digital twin-driven operations enable predictive optimization.
trends.actions_md:
1. Develop digital twin models.
2. Deploy sensor networks.
3. Build scenario planning capabilities.

trends.name: Zero-Trust Sicherheitsarchitektur
trends.evidence_md: Zero-trust replaces perimeter-based systems. Grid cybersecurity market projected to reach $29.70 billion by 2034 [Precedence Research](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/grid-cybersecurity-market).
trends.implications_md: Zero-trust requires fundamental redesign of network security.
trends.opportunities_md: Zero-trust enables secure remote operations and cloud adoption for KRITIS.
trends.actions_md:
1. Redesign security architecture.
2. Implement continuous monitoring.
3. Deploy identity-centric access control.
